raw_consumer_statement: I had a positive balance of {$80.00} on my credit card account. I made a few purchases totaling {$82.00} and noticed that instead of my balance being {$2.00}, it had doubled to {$160.00}. When I contacted the bank, they told me that they had mailed me a check for {$80.00} on XX/XX/year> and when I received it I would be able to pay the balance. The check should arrive within 5-7 business days.I had a positive balance of {$80.00} on my credit card account. I made some purchases totaling {$82.00} and noticed that instead of my balance being {$2.00}, it was doubled to {$160.00}. When I contacted the bank I was informed that they had mailed me a check for {$80.00} on XX/XX/year> and when I received it I would be able to pay the balance. The check should arrive within XXXX business days. However, they did not even notify me that a check was mailed to me. I did not receive this check and contacted the bank again on XX/XX/year>. They informed me that they would cancel the previous check and send a new one, which I would have to wait for another XXXX (! ) business days to pay a debt that I essentially do not owe. In addition, there must be a due date that must occur before the expected check arrives and I need to find the money to avoid paying penalties. I asked if there was another way to get my money back? I was told that they can cancel the check and transfer the money to the account, which will take XXXX business days. Finally, after 3 days, I saw this money {$80.00} on my account, paid the missing {$2.00} so that the balance was {$0.00}. But today I see the balance has become the same {$80.00} (! ). My question is- what games does the bank play with its clients? Please, return my money back ASAP and close my account. Thanks
